channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: US-Philippines Deploy Anti-Ship Missile System Near Taiwan in War Games
item.description: Philippine and US forces showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes province near Taiwan during annual military exercises, as regional tensions over the self-governed island continue to escalate.

item.title: OPEC+ Agrees Symbolic Oil Output Hike Despite Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ agreed in principle to a small oil output quota hike, though the increase will remain largely symbolic until shipping through the Strait of Hormuz can resume amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

item.title: US Approves $8.6 Billion in Arms Sales to Middle East Allies
item.description: The United States has approved $8.6 billion in weapons sales to Middle Eastern allies amid the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, with the White House fast-tracking sales and bypassing Congressional review.

item.title: Trump Reviews Iranian Peace Proposal, Warns Strikes Could Resume
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he is reviewing a 14-point peace proposal from Iran but expressed skepticism it would succeed, warning that US strikes could resume if Tehran "misbehaves." Iran submitted the proposal via Pakistani mediators.

item.title: Millions of Jobs Lost as Iran Battles "Operation Economic Fury" Fallout
item.description: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is devastating Tehran, with millions of jobs lost and crippling effects on daily life from the sustained military and economic pressure campaign.

item.title: Taiwan President Defiant as China Calls Him a "Rat"
item.description: Taiwan has a right to engage with the world and no country can stop that, President Lai Ching-te told Eswatini's king after arriving for a surprise trip that Taipei says Beijing tried to stop, as China condemned him as a "rat".

item.title: German Minister: US Troop Drawdown Should Spur European Defense Responsibility
item.description: The Pentagon announced the withdrawal of 5,000 troops from Germany as a rift over the Iran war and tariff tensions placed more strain on relations between the U.S. and Europe.

item.title: Nobel Laureate Mohammadi Hospitalized After Cardiac Crisis in Iran
item.description: The head of the Norwegian Nobel Committee said the life of jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi was in the hands of the Iranian authorities after her health "deteriorated seriously", and called for her release to receive proper medical care.

item.title: Iran Standoff Could Leave Trump Worse Off Than Before He Went to War
item.description: A month after the U.S. and Israel paused their bombing campaign against Iran, the two sides appear no closer to a deal to end the war, with Trump facing mounting pressure as the conflict continues without resolution.

item.title: Trump Auto Tariff Hike Could Cost Germany Nearly $18 Billion in Output
item.description: The tariff hike on cars and trucks from the European Union announced by U.S. President Donald Trump could cost Germany nearly 15 billion euros ($17.58 billion) in output, an economic institute told Reuters on Saturday.

item.title: Reclusive Turkmenistan Shows Signs of Cautiously Opening Up
item.description: From a brightly lit, open-plan office Azat Seyitmuhammedov runs an e-commerce startup, Wabrum, that he founded almost a decade ago. The isolated Central Asian nation is beginning to show signs of economic reform and opening.

item.title: Iraq Says Oil Output Can Recover Within Week Once Hormuz Crisis Ends
item.description: Iraq can restore oil output and exports to normal levels within seven days of the end of the crisis over the Strait of Hormuz, Deputy Oil Minister Basim Mohammed said on Saturday, as the country plans for post-conflict recovery.

item.title: Japan's Taiyo Oil to Receive Cargo From Russia's Sakhalin-2 Project
item.description: Japan's Taiyo Oil will receive a cargo of oil from Russia's Sakhalin-2 project, highlighting continued energy ties despite geopolitical tensions and sanctions on Russian energy exports.

item.title: Cambodia Court Convicts Opposition Leader Kem Sokha of Treason
item.description: A Cambodian court convicted opposition leader Kem Sokha of treason, a decision rights groups condemned as politically motivated to silence dissent ahead of elections.

item.title: CNN Analysis: After Two Months, Iran War Has No Clear Winner
item.description: A CNN analysis examines how the US-Iran conflict has evolved over two months without a decisive victory, with both sides locked in a stalemate amid economic pressure and military posturing.

item.title: US Inflation Expectations Back to a Boil Amid Iran War Stasis
item.description: The ongoing stalemate in the Iran conflict is bringing U.S. inflation expectations back to concerning levels, as energy markets remain uncertain and supply chain disruptions persist.

item.title: US Navy Turns to AI Firm for Options to Counter Iranian Mines
item.description: The US Navy is partnering with AI company Domino to develop countermeasures against Iranian naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz, as the waterway remains a critical chokepoint in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: China's Gravity-Detecting "Squid" Gets Closer to Spotting US Nuclear Submarines
item.description: Chinese researchers are developing gravity-detecting technology that could help identify US nuclear submarines, potentially shifting the strategic balance in undersea warfare.

item.title: BBC: Middle East States Eye Transport Resilience With New Logistics Corridor Bypassing Hormuz
item.description: Regional powers are exploring alternative transport routes to bypass the Strait of Hormuz as the blockade continues, seeking long-term supply chain resilience amid ongoing instability.

item.title: Al Jazeera: Mali Probes Soldiers Suspected of Involvement in Military Base Attacks
item.description: Malian authorities are investigating soldiers suspected of involvement in attacks on military bases, highlighting internal security challenges in the Sahel region.

item.title: The Guardian: Zambia Cancels RightsCon 2026 Summit
item.description: Zambia has cancelled the RightsCon 2026 summit, a major digital rights conference, raising concerns about the country's commitment to internet freedom and civil society spaces.

item.title: South China Morning Post: Middle East States Eye Transport Resilience With New Corridor Bypassing Hormuz
item.description: Middle Eastern states are pursuing new logistics corridors to bypass the Strait of Hormuz as regional powers seek supply chain resilience during the ongoing crisis.

item.title: Korea Herald: South Korea Eyes Energy Security Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: South Korea is reassessing its energy security strategies as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to impact global oil supplies and shipping routes.

item.title: Deutsche Welle: Iran War Live Updates as Trump Reviews New Tehran Proposal
item.description: Live coverage of the Iran conflict as President Trump reviews a new proposal from Tehran with skepticism, while diplomatic efforts continue to resolve the stalemate.
